item.title: Naga Malladi’s Breakthrough in Product Management Excellence
item.author: Kashvi Pandey
item.content:encoded: \ In an era of rapid digital transformation and increasing enterprise software complexity, Naga Srirama Narasimha Raviteja Malladi has emerged as a transformative force at a leading global technology corporation. His strategic leadership has generated multi-million dollar value while revolutionizing product delivery and customer experience for millions of users. Through innovative approaches to process optimization and stakeholder management, Naga has set new benchmarks for excellence in enterprise software development and delivery.\ At the heart of this technology giant's operational transformation lies Naga's innovative approach to complex enterprise engineering planning processes. Recognizing the challenges of cross-functional collaboration in a large enterprise environment, he spearheaded the implementation of a groundbreaking continuous planning methodology. This initiative fundamentally reshaped how diverse business units collaborate and align their objectives, achieving a remarkable 30% increase in project efficiency while reducing planning cycle times by 25%. The continuous planning framework introduced by Naga addressed several critical challenges in enterprise software development. By establishing clear communication channels and standardized processes across teams, he eliminated traditional silos that often impede progress in large organizations. The framework's success has made it a model for other divisions within the corporation, demonstrating the scalability and effectiveness of his approach to organizational change.\ Naga Malladi's expertise particularly shines in his management of complex international regulatory compliance and finance projects for e-commerce consumer products. In an environment where regulatory requirements grow increasingly complex, he successfully navigated the intricate landscape of compliance while maintaining focus on business growth and operational efficiency. His strategic approach to revenue recognition and financial reporting resulted in a 20% improvement in accuracy and a 15% reduction in processing time. These improvements went beyond mere technical enhancements. By implementing robust compliance frameworks and streamlined financial processes, Naga established new standards for regulatory excellence in enterprise software delivery. His ability to balance to comply strictly with the evolving regulatory requirements with operational efficiency has become a benchmark for similar initiatives across the corporation's product portfolio.\ Naga's success in driving organizational change relied heavily on his exceptional stakeholder management skills. By fostering strong relationships across diverse business units and maintaining clear communication channels with senior executives, he created an environment conducive to transformation and innovation. His approach to stakeholder engagement has become a model for cross-functional leadership within organizations. The impact of his stakeholder management strategy extended beyond immediate project teams. By aligning diverse business objectives and maintaining transparent communication, Naga ensured that all stakeholders remained engaged and committed to project success. This approach proved particularly valuable in managing complex initiatives that required coordination across multiple organizational boundaries.\ Throughout his product management tenure, Naga has maintained an unwavering focus on customer value. His initiatives have directly impacted millions of users, improving their experience while ensuring the highest standards of regulatory compliance and operational excellence. This customer-centric approach has been fundamental to the success of his projects and has influenced how large corporations approach product development. By integrating customer feedback and market insights into the development process, Naga has ensured that technical improvements align with real user needs. This approach has not only enhanced customer satisfaction rates significantly, but has also contributed to the substantial financial value generated by his initiatives.\ The financial impact of Naga's leadership has been significant, with his initiatives generating multi -million dollar value for his customers. This success stems from his ability to optimize operations, reduce costs, and improve efficiency across multiple business functions. His strategic approach to resource allocation and process improvement has consistently delivered measurable results, exceeding traditional performance metrics. Looking ahead, the implications of Naga's success extend well beyond immediate achievements. His work has established new standards for enterprise product management, demonstrating how strategic leadership can transform complex organizations while delivering exceptional value to stakeholders.\ His influence on the approach to product management, stakeholder engagement, and regulatory compliance continues to shape how organizations develop and deliver enterprise software solutions. The principles and practices he established have become integral to the product management philosophy, influencing how teams approach complex challenges and deliver value to customers.About Naga Srirama Narasimha Raviteja MalladiA distinguished professional in enterprise product management, Naga has established himself as a leading expert in cross-functional leadership and process optimization. His comprehensive experience spans regulatory compliance, product strategy, financial systems, and large-scale organizational change management. With proven expertise in stakeholder alignment and strategic planning, Naga has consistently delivered operational excellence while ensuring compliance with complex regulatory requirements.\ His innovative approach to continuous planning and process optimization has set new standards for enterprise product management, making him a recognized leader in the product management community. Naga Malladi's ability to bridge technical, business, and regulatory requirements while maintaining focus on customer value has established him as a transformative force in enterprise software development. His strategic approach to stakeholder management and organizational change continues to influence how enterprise software products are developed and delivered, setting new benchmarks for excellence in the industry.:::tip This story was distributed as a release by Kashvi Pandey under HackerNoon’s Business Blogging Program. Learn more about the program .

item.title: Gemini hackers can deliver more potent attacks with a helping hand from… Gemini
item.author: Dan Goodin
item.content:encoded: In the growing canon of AI security, the indirect prompt injection has emerged as the most powerful means for attackers to hack large language models such as OpenAI’s GPT-3 and GPT-4 or Microsoft’s Copilot. By exploiting a model's inability to distinguish between, on the one hand, developer-defined prompts and, on the other, text in external content LLMs interact with, indirect prompt injections are remarkably effective at invoking harmful or otherwise unintended actions. Examples include divulging end users’ confidential contacts or emails and delivering falsified answers that have the potential to corrupt the integrity of important calculations.Despite the power of prompt injections, attackers face a fundamental challenge in using them: The inner workings of so-called closed-weights models such as GPT, Anthropic’s Claude, and Google’s Gemini are closely held secrets. Developers of such proprietary platforms tightly restrict access to the underlying code and training data that make them work and, in the process, make them black boxes to external users. As a result, devising working prompt injections requires labor- and time-intensive trial and error through redundant manual effort.Algorithmically generated hacksFor the first time, academic researchers have devised a means to create computer-generated prompt injections against Gemini that have much higher success rates than manually crafted ones. The new method abuses fine-tuning, a feature offered by some closed-weights models for training them to work on large amounts of private or specialized data, such as a law firm’s legal case files, patient files or research managed by a medical facility, or architectural blueprints. Google makes its fine-tuning for Gemini’s API available free of charge.
